name: financials_classify_amazon_statement_transactions
description:
Aggregates Amazon settlement transactions using one of two classification modes.

Two modes:

1. **default** (CoA-based) — Maps each transaction's service_name to the company's Chart of Accounts via the services table (services.name → accounts via income_account_id, with parent account chain). Returns totals grouped by account_classification (1.Revenue/2.Expense/3.Asset/…), account_type (1.Income/2.Cost of Goods Sold/3.Expense/…), and account_name. This is the standard bookkeeping view aligned with the company's actual CoA.

2. **reference** — Uses NeonPanel's built-in reference classification (settlement_flat_mapping → settlement_subclasses → settlement_classes). Returns totals grouped by class/subclass codes. This is a fixed, pre-defined classification hierarchy maintained by NeonPanel:
   - '1' = Income, '2' = Amazon Fees, '3' = Cost of Advertising, '4' = Tax, '5' = Other
   - Subclass examples: '1.01' = Product Sales, '2.01' = Referral Fees, etc.

Output: One row per classification group (optionally per marketplace or settlement), with total debit, credit, net amount, line count.

Use this tool to:
- Get a classified summary of settlement data for a given period
- Compare classified totals against an uploaded Amazon payments report (PDF)
- View income vs. fees vs. tax breakdown (reference mode) or by CoA accounts (default mode)
- Drill into account-level or subclass-level detail
- Add marketplace or settlement granularity to the breakdown

Sign convention: positive = credit to seller (income), negative = charge (fee/tax). total_debit and total_credit split debits and credits into separate unsigned totals.

Reconciliation workflow:
1. User uploads a PDF payments report → LLM extracts dates & amounts
2. LLM calls THIS tool with matching date range → gets classified totals
3. LLM compares PDF amounts vs. classified totals per category
4. LLM calls financials_save_amazon_statement_reconciliation_result to persist the comparison
5. Use financials_list_amazon_statement_reconciliation_results to review saved results later

ProTip: Use financials_list_amazon_statements first to find settlement IDs and date ranges, then filter by settlement_ids here for precise matching.

Integration:
- Cross-reference with financials_analyze_amazon_statement for raw transaction-level detail
- Use financials_analyze_service_coa_mapping to inspect or fix service → account mappings
- Use financials_list_chart_of_accounts to browse the full CoA

Default time range: Last 3 months when no dates are provided.

Knowledge base: https://help.neonpanel.com
Dig deeper: For a full interactive settlement view, direct the user to https://my.neonpanel.com

query.description: Query envelope for classified statement transactions.

mode.description:
Classification mode.

- 'default': CoA-based — maps service_name → services → accounts → account_types. Groups by account_classification, account_type, account_name. Use this for bookkeeping reconciliation aligned with the company's Chart of Accounts.
- 'reference': NeonPanel's built-in reference classification (class/subclass hierarchy). Use this for standardized comparison across companies or when CoA mapping is incomplete.

filters.description: Filters for the query. company_id is required; all others are optional.

settlement_ids.description: Filter by specific settlement IDs. Use values from financials_list_amazon_statements.

marketplace_codes.description: Filter by marketplace country code (e.g., 'US', 'DE', 'SE', 'GB', 'FR', 'IT', 'ES'). Derived from amazon_sellers + amazon_marketplaces. Case-insensitive.

class_codes.description: [reference mode only] Filter by class code: '1' (Income), '2' (Amazon Fees), '3' (Cost of Advertising), '4' (Tax), '5' (Other). Ignored in default mode.

subclass_codes.description: [reference mode only] Filter by subclass code: '1.01', '2.01', '2.02', etc. Ignored in default mode.

account_classifications.description: [default mode only] Filter by account classification (e.g., 'Revenue', 'Expense', 'Asset', 'Liability', 'Equity'). Ignored in reference mode.

account_types.description: [default mode only] Filter by account type name (e.g., 'Income', 'Expense', 'Cost of Goods Sold', 'Other Income', 'Other Expense'). Ignored in reference mode.

account_names.description: [default mode only] Filter by account name (substring match, case-insensitive). Ignored in reference mode.

time.description: Date range filter on transaction_date (the date each transaction was posted).

start_date.description: Start of date range (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 3 months ago if omitted.

end_date.description: End of date range (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today if omitted.

aggregation.description: Controls aggregation granularity. Available dimensions depend on mode.

group_by.description:
Dimensions to group by.

Default mode dimensions (CoA-based):
- account_classification: top-level classification (Revenue / Expense / Asset / …)
- account_type: account type name (Income / Expense / Cost of Goods Sold / …)
- account_name: individual account name from the CoA
- service_name: invoice-level service label
- marketplace: marketplace country code + name
- settlement: individual settlement ID
Default for default mode: ['account_classification', 'account_type', 'account_name']

Reference mode dimensions:
- class: NeonPanel class (Income / Amazon Fees / Cost of Advertising / Tax / Other)
- subclass: NeonPanel subclass (Product Sales / Referral Fees / …)
- service_name: invoice-level service label
- marketplace: marketplace country code + name
- settlement: individual settlement ID
Default for reference mode: ['class', 'subclass']

sort.description: Controls ranking and ordering of results.

field.description: Column to sort by. Default mode supports: account_classification, account_type, account_name, total_amount, total_debit, total_credit, line_count. Reference mode supports: class_code, subclass_code, total_amount, total_debit, total_credit, line_count.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., 'EUR', 'GBP', 'USD'). Always present — amounts are never mixed across currencies.

account_classification.description: [default mode] Normalized top-level classification: '1.Revenue', '2.Expense', '3.Asset', '4.Liability'. Present in default mode when grouped by account_classification.

account_type.description: [default mode] Normalized account type (e.g., '1.Income', '2.Cost of Goods Sold', '3.Expense', '4.Other Expense'). Present in default mode when grouped by account_type.

account_name.description: [default mode] Full account path from the company's CoA including number and parent chain (e.g., '4000 Sales: Amazon Sales'). Present in default mode when grouped by account_name.

mapping_status.description: [default mode] Service-to-account mapping status: 'mapped' (service found + account resolved in CoA), 'unmapped' (no matching service or no income_account_id). Present in default mode.

class_code.description: [reference mode] Accounting class code (e.g., '1', '2'). Present in reference mode when grouped by class.

class_name.description: [reference mode] Accounting class name (e.g., 'Income', 'Amazon Fees'). Present in reference mode when grouped by class.

subclass_code.description: [reference mode] Accounting subclass code (e.g., '1.01', '2.01'). Present in reference mode when grouped by subclass.

subclass_name.description: [reference mode] Accounting subclass name (e.g., 'Product Sales', 'Referral Fees'). Present in reference mode when grouped by subclass.

service_name.description: Invoice-level service label. Present when grouped by service_name.

marketplace_code.description: Marketplace country code (e.g., 'US', 'DE'). Present when grouped by marketplace.

marketplace_name.description: Marketplace name (e.g., 'Amazon.de'). Present when grouped by marketplace.

settlement_id.description: Settlement identifier. Present when grouped by settlement.

total_amount.description: Net amount (debit − credit) in statement currency.

total_amount_usd.description: Net amount converted to USD via currency_rates. 0 if rate unavailable.

total_amount_main.description: Net amount converted to the company's main currency (via USD→main rate). NULL if rate unavailable.

main_currency.description: The company's main currency code (e.g., 'EUR', 'GBP'). Used for total_amount_main conversion.

examples.title: CoA-based summary (default mode)
examples.description: Classify settlement transactions by Chart of Accounts: account classification → account type → account name.

examples.description: Amazon Fees broken down by marketplace (reference mode).
